bullets.text: Israeli soldiers have been punished for vandalizing a Jesus statue in Lebanon, with two removed from combat duty and receiving 30 days of military detention.
Taiwan's president, Lai Ching-te, cancelled a trip to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius, and Madagascar revoked overflight permission, which Taiwan blames on China.
Two US officials reportedly working for the CIA died in a car crash in Mexico following a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab, prompting an investigation into the US's role.
US President Donald Trump has extended a ceasefire with Iran, stating he will hold off on military action until Tehran presents a unified proposal for a peace deal.
Trump's decision to extend the ceasefire comes after an earlier threat to resume bombing, with Pakistani officials reportedly requesting the extension.
Despite the ceasefire extension, a military blockade on Iranian ports is set to continue, and pressure is building for peace talks to progress.
President Trump has extended a ceasefire with Iran until talks conclude, despite Iranian officials expressing doubt about negotiations and accusing the US of violating the truce.
In defiance of a US blockade, dozens of Iranian tankers, some laden with oil, have bypassed restrictions and exited the Gulf.
Energy markets remain volatile with oil and gas prices fluctuating, as the extended ceasefire has not fully eased concerns over the Strait of Hormuz blockade.
